title: athlete-guardian — field classification
description: AthleteGuardian carries mostly public-tier (structural) + internal-tier (verification state) fields. Notes + dispute_reason + revoke_reason are confidential-tier (HR-visible only). Guardian names + emails cascade the redactor treatment from their User (regulated_minor tier when the underlying athlete is a minor).

regulated_minor: For guardians of MINOR athletes: the guardian's User first_name + last_name are STRIPPED from cross-tenant platform-admin reads (minor-adjacent data protection). Guardian's own User.email is redacted to domain-only for the same audience.

notes: Guardian rows themselves carry no PII fields — the PII lives in the linked User + Profile. The redactor pipeline for user data applies transitively when a guardian row is read through Guardian.user->profile.

For minor athletes, the guardian's name + email are subject to the same regulated_minor stripping as the athlete's — cross-tenant platform-admin reads never expose the guardian's identity when the underlying athlete is a minor.
Guardian.notes + dispute_reason + revoke_reason are HR-visible internal fields — they do NOT surface on the tenant activity feed. Only the FACT that a dispute / revoke happened is audit-visible.
